description:
## Parent

bsm-7en — Render selected issue details in the issue explorer

## Background and intent

Earlier beads in this epic should include fast unit, adapter/RPC/Effect, and component/integration tests where possible. This final bead adds the real desktop proof: launch the built Tauri app against a disposable Beadwork workspace, click an Issue in the Issue List, and verify that selected Issue Detail content appears through the full Rust adapter → TauRPC → Effect → React path.

The existing Issue List WebDriver suite already launches the real debug desktop binary rather than a renderer-only mock. This bead should extend that suite instead of creating a parallel E2E surface. The test should remain focused on one representative user path and avoid brittle visual assertions.

This bead also cleans up documentation so future agents see the post-epic vocabulary and test behavior: `Issue` / `list_issues`, selectable Issue List, and Issue Detail rendering.

## What to build

Extend the existing WebDriver Issue List fixture and success scenario so it creates a disposable Beadwork workspace with an Issue that has enough data to prove detail rendering after selection. The scenario should launch the real desktop app, wait for the Issue List, click an Issue row, and assert representative Issue Detail content is visible.

The E2E assertion set should prove the completed path without overfitting implementation details. Representative content should include enough of the completed feature to catch integration regressions, such as selected title/ID, Markdown description output, dependency context or metadata, and comment content.

Update WebDriver/E2E documentation and stale summary-oriented references so future agents understand the current suite and naming.

## Reasoning and constraints

- Keep E2E as its own final slice, per planning decision, because earlier slices can be verified faster with unit/component/integration tests.
- Extend the existing Issue List suite rather than creating a new suite unless the existing structure makes that impossible.
- The suite should launch the real built desktop binary using the existing WebDriver/debug build setup.
- Avoid asserting CSS implementation details or fragile layout internals. Assert user-visible outcomes.
- Existing empty-list E2E behavior must continue to pass.
- Do not add new product behavior in this bead; this is proof and documentation for the completed Issue Detail path.

## Testing guidance

Run the relevant WebDriver Issue List commands documented in the repo after updating the fixture/spec. If the upstream WebDriver caveats from the existing docs still apply, preserve or update those notes rather than rediscovering them later.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The existing Issue List WebDriver fixture includes an Issue description and comments suitable for detail-rendering assertions.
- [ ] The WebDriver success scenario launches the real desktop app against a disposable Beadwork workspace.
- [ ] The success scenario clicks an Issue row and verifies representative Issue Detail content renders through the full Rust adapter → TauRPC → Effect → React path.
- [ ] The E2E test stays focused on user-visible outcomes and avoids over-asserting visual implementation details.
- [ ] Existing empty-list E2E behavior continues to pass.
- [ ] WebDriver/E2E documentation is updated for the selected Issue Detail path, including how to run the suite and any known caveats that still apply.
- [ ] Stale `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` references in relevant docs are updated to `Issue` / `list_issues` language.
- [ ] No new application behavior is introduced only to satisfy the E2E test.

## Out of scope

- No new Issue Detail features beyond proving those already built by previous beads.
- No renderer-only E2E replacement for the real desktop path.
- No broad visual regression suite.
- No new workspace-switching, refresh, route, or mutation behavior.

## Blocked by

bsm-7en.2
bsm-7en.3
bsm-7en.4
bsm-7en.5
title: Extend desktop E2E coverage for Issue Detail
